questions.question: According to Roman legend, who was the first king and founder of Rome?
Which conflict, consisting of three separate wars, was fought between Rome and Carthage?
What was the name of the legislative body of the Roman Republic, composed primarily of aristocrats?
Who led the Carthaginian force that famously crossed the Alps with war elephants to invade Italy?
What was the term for the common citizens of Rome, distinct from the patricians?
Which Roman leader was assassinated on the Ides of March in 44 BCE?
Who was the first official Emperor of Rome, reigning from 27 BCE to 14 CE?
What was the long period of relative peace and stability across the Roman Empire called?
Which architectural structure was used by Romans to transport water from distant sources into cities?
Which Emperor famously converted to Christianity?
What was the primary tactical unit of the Roman army, typically consisting of 4,000 to 6,000 soldiers?
Which Roman city was buried and preserved by the eruption of Mount Vesuvius in 79 CE?
What was the name of the defensive fortification built across northern Britain by the Romans?
Which set of laws, inscribed on bronze, formed the foundation of Roman law?
What was the Circus Maximus primarily used for in ancient Rome?
Which Emperor is notoriously remembered for his eccentricities and... possibly... making his horse a candidate for office?
Where was the capital of the Eastern Roman Empire located?
What was the title given to the two highest-ranking elected officials who governed the Republic?
Which slave and former gladiator led a massive rebellion against the Roman Republic in 73 BCE?
What is the name of the massive amphitheater used for gladiatorial contests?
Which of the following was true about the Roman Empire?
True or False?:

events: Sack of Rome by the Gauls
places: A city made entirely of parmesan

facts: Purple was a valuable color because it was made from thousands of sea snails

Romans viewed left-handedness as evil and unlucky
misconceptions: Julius Caesar was born via C-section
Emperor Augustus was actually three children in a trench coat
Romulus and Remus were raised by a leopard
Romans used vomitoriums to throw up during feasts
Romans wore white togas every day
The Thumbs Down meant death
Et tu, Brute? were Caesars actual last words
The Senate was a democratic body
Caligula made his horse a Consul
Aqueducts were used as water slides
The Senate House was used as a bird sanctuary
Mount Vesuvius erupted for the drama
The Circus Maximus was used for unicycle races
Romans salted the earth of Carthage
All gladiators fought to the death
The Punic Wars were fought over a bad joke
Romans saluted with a straight arm
Hannibal crossed the Alps on a lion
Gladiators fought using giant rubber tree branches
The Fall of Rome happened in a single day
Christianity was the sole cause of Romes collapse
Nero played the fiddle during the Great Fire
Julius Caesar’s favorite food was the Caesar Salad
The Fall of Rome was caused by a shortage of salted meat
